headline: Cleveland Fed sees AI fueling inflation, rate hikes may be needed
detail: This is directly hostile to your book. Rate hike expectations crush growth and tech multiples hardest - chips, AI names, and leveraged funds like SOXL all sell off fast when rate fears spike. On margin, that pain is amplified immediately.
rec: No specific new buy here - this is a warning to reduce risk. If SOXL or SMH show weakness on the open, trimming SOXL toward a smaller size is worth considering given the 3x leverage exposure.

headline: Trump AI crackdown opens door for China to close gap
detail: Regulatory headwinds on AI exports hurt US semiconductor and chip equipment names - exactly your heaviest exposure via SMH, SOXL, and CRWD adjacent names. If China accelerates its own chip development, it pressures long-term US chip dominance narratives that have driven these valuations.
rec: No action today, but watch SMH price action closely. If SMH breaks below recent support, SOXL becomes a trim candidate given its 3x decay risk on a sustained sector pullback.

headline: Sandisk analysts see another big quarter ahead
detail: Positive analyst sentiment on a storage/semiconductor name is a modest tailwind for chip sector mood, supporting SMH and names like KLAC and AMAT in your scanner. It's a narrow read but confirms underlying demand signals in parts of the chip space.
rec: KLAC has a scanner setup (entry 293.72-294.68, stop 291.83) and is a grade-A daily/4H/1H alignment, but it's in daily premium meaning you'd be chasing. Only consider if price pulls into that entry zone cleanly - do not chase above it.

headline: Small-cap stocks best first half in 35 years
detail: IWM and TNA are both in the scanner as grade-A long setups, and small-cap momentum is a real fundamental tailwind right now. This is one of the few headlines with a direct scanner match and a macro reason behind it.
rec: IWM scanner entry is 298.92-299.22, stop 298.26 - very tight stop. If you want small-cap exposure, IWM is cleaner than TNA (TNA is leveraged, adds risk on top of your already margin-heavy book). Consider IWM only at the scanner entry zone, not above it.
